title: Kevladev National Park
tagline: Kevladev National Park — wildlife reserve of Rajasthan

overview.short: Keoladeo National Park, or Keoladeo Ghana National Park, is a national park in Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Keoladeo National Park, or Keoladeo Ghana National Park, is a national park in Rajasthan, India. The national park hosts thousands of native, resident and migratory birds, especially during the winter season, when many different species fly to the Indian subcontinent to escape harsh winters further north in Eurasia. At least 400 avian species have been noted or observed in the national park. The area was developed into a duck shooting reserve in 1899 by the administrator of the Bharatpur State.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chak Kazi Barrpura
topPlaces.description: Chak Kazi Barrpura (also spelled as 'Barrpura', 'Barrapura', 'Chak Qazi', Chak Kazi or 'Chak Kaji') is a small village about 12 km from Bharatpur, India in Rajasthan state. This village is located beside the Keoladeo National Park (KNP) in south-west side of its boundary.

topPlaces.name: Bijwari
topPlaces.description: Bijwari is a village in Wair tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. It is a village of Indolia gotra Jats.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Bharatpur (1805)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Bharatpur took place between 2 January and 22 February 1805 in the Indian Princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan), during the Second Anglo-Maratha War. Forces of the British East India Company, led by General Gerard Lake, were repulsed four times in their attempts to storm the fortress.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Bharatpur (1825–1826)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Bharatpur was a siege that took place in the Indian princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan) between December 1825 and January 1826. British troops under Lord Combermere initially surrounded the state's capital until on 18 January 1826 its fortress was stormed and captured.

topPlaces.name: Lohagarh Fort
topPlaces.description: Lohagarh Fort (transl. Iron Fort) is an 18th-century fort located at Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India.Maharaja Suraj Mal commissioned its construction in 1732.

topPlaces.name: Bharatpur State
topPlaces.description: Bharatpur was a tributary of the House of Scindia, and later a princely state under British suzerainty. It was ruled by the Sinsinwar clan of the Hindu Jats.

topPlaces.name: Bharatpur, Rajasthan
topPlaces.description: Bharatpur is a city in the Indian state of Rajasthan, 189 km (117 mi) south of India's capital, New Delhi, 184 km (114 mi) from Rajasthan's capital Jaipur, 56 km (35 mi) west of Agra of Uttar Pradesh and 36 km (22 mi) from Mathura of Uttar Pradesh. It is the administrative headquarters of Bharatpur District and the headquarters of Bharatpur Division of Rajasthan State.

topPlaces.name: Bharatpur Junction
topPlaces.description: Bharatpur Junction is a railway station located on the Mathura–Kota section of the New Delhi–Mumbai main line of Indian Railways in Bharatpur, Rajasthan. It is an important station of the West Central Railway zone (WCR) and has the status of Grade A station.

items.note: Chak Kazi Barrpura (also spelled as 'Barrpura', 'Barrapura', 'Chak Qazi', Chak Kazi or 'Chak Kaji') is a small village about 12 km from Bharatpur, India in Rajasthan state. This village is located beside the Keoladeo National Park (KNP) in south-west side of its boundary.
Bijwari is a village in Wair tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. It is a village of Indolia gotra Jats.

The siege of Bharatpur took place between 2 January and 22 February 1805 in the Indian Princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan), during the Second Anglo-Maratha War. Forces of the British East India Company, led by General Gerard Lake, were repulsed four times in their attempts to storm the fortress.

The siege of Bharatpur was a siege that took place in the Indian princely state of Bharatpur (now part of Rajasthan) between December 1825 and January 1826. British troops under Lord Combermere initially surrounded the state's capital until on 18 January 1826 its fortress was stormed and captured.
Lohagarh Fort (transl. Iron Fort) is an 18th-century fort located at Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India.Maharaja Suraj Mal commissioned its construction in 1732.
Bharatpur was a tributary of the House of Scindia, and later a princely state under British suzerainty. It was ruled by the Sinsinwar clan of the Hindu Jats.

Bharatpur is a city in the Indian state of Rajasthan, 189 km (117 mi) south of India's capital, New Delhi, 184 km (114 mi) from Rajasthan's capital Jaipur, 56 km (35 mi) west of Agra of Uttar Pradesh and 36 km (22 mi) from Mathura of Uttar Pradesh. It is the administrative headquarters of Bharatpur District and the headquarters of Bharatpur Division of Rajasthan State.

Bharatpur Junction is a railway station located on the Mathura–Kota section of the New Delhi–Mumbai main line of Indian Railways in Bharatpur, Rajasthan. It is an important station of the West Central Railway zone (WCR) and has the status of Grade A station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kevladev National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kevladev National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kevladev National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (44 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kevladev National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kevladev National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kevladev National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Kevladev National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kevladev National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chak Kazi Barrpura, Bijwari, Siege of Bharatpur (1805), Siege of Bharatpur (1825–1826), Lohagarh Fort.
